creator: wosch@cs.tu-berlin.de
text:
Stephen J. Roznowski writes:
>	Executing "man -k xyz" generates the following:
>
>	xyz: nothing appropriate
>	Error executing formatting or display command.
>	system command exited with status 256
>
>	Everything after the first line appears extraneous.

This is a side effect of last apropos(1) changes. Now apropos(1)
exit status is 0 if matches where found and 1 if no matches were
found. The exit status 1 of apropos command confuse man(1).

-- Wolfram Schneider <wosch@FreeBSD.org>
